office_does: A PUD commissioner sets policy for the customer-owned utility, including rates, reliability, resource planning, and utility services.
race_blurb: Incumbent Sid Logan faces Bruce King and former Island County commissioner Janet St Clair in a race centered on rates, reliability, resource planning, and clean-energy investment.

candidates.summary: Logan is the incumbent PUD commissioner, emphasizing rates, reliability, cybersecurity, renewable integration, and customer assistance.
highlights: Has served as PUD commissioner since 2017 and is an engineer and Energy Northwest director [S1]
Supports low rates, reliability, cyber preparation, renewable integration, and customer assistance [S1][S2]

candidates.summary: King is a technology entrepreneur and farmer emphasizing low-cost reliable service, broad resource options, and greater public input.
highlights: Supports planning across nuclear, solar, hydro, and fossil options with greater public input [S1][S2]

candidates.summary: St Clair is a former Island County commissioner emphasizing affordability, reliable service, and investment in water and energy resources.
highlights: Served eight years as Island County commissioner and previously led nonprofits [S1]
Promises low bills and reliable service alongside expanded water and energy investment [S1][S2]
